DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT HAMILTON FAMILIES' MOTION FOR ORDER TO DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED AND FOR AN ORDER IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS. THE UNOPPOSED MOTION IS GRANTED AND THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION AT ISSUE IN THE MOTION ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PAY MONETARY SANCTIONS TO DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $430, DUE TO DEFENDANT WITHIN TEN (10) CALENDAR DAYS. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING THE COURT'S RULING VERBATIM AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT JPDEOL@DHILLONLAW.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: JOHN-PAUL DEOL, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-19-2023 Vacated

Notice Of Motion For Order To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Defendant's Motion For Order To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Transferred to be heard on the court's discovery calendar (room 301) on the same date at 9:00 a.m.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEFENDANT HAMILTON FAMILIES' MOTION FOR ORDER TO DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED AND FOR AN ORDER IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS (TRANSFERRED FROM THE LAW & MOTION CALENDAR TO THE DISCOVERY CALENDAR)
Off Calendar Oct-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.*** The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HAMILTON FAMILIES' MOTION FOR ORDER TO DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED AND FOR AN ORDER IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS.

The unopposed motion is granted and the requests for admission at issue in the motion are deemed admitted. Plaintiff's counsel shall pay monetary sanctions to Defendant in the amount of $430, due to Defendant within ten (10) calendar days.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating the Court's ruling verbatim and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at JPDeol@dhillonlaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

***At 9:15am Mr. Enrique Ramirez appears late for the laintiff after the calling of the case. The JPT informs counsel he did not receive any contest emails and informs counsel of the Court's ruling.***
